prs.title: [MoE] Introduce Fp8PrepMixin and class-based dispatch for DeepGemm Fp8
prs.author: yzong-rh
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

vLLM's fused MoE (Mixture-of-Experts) layer uses a three-stage pipeline to set up quantized expert kernels at model-load time:
- **MoEMethod** (e.g. `Fp8MoEMethod`, `CompressedTensorsW8A8Fp8MoEMethod`, `ModelOptFp8MoEMethod`) — the quantization strategy. It registers weight/scale parameters and orchestrates the load-time pipeline (weight prep → quant config → kernel creation)
- **Oracle** (`oracle/fp8.py`) — the backend-selection layer. It chooses which GPU kernel implementation (DeepGemm, Triton, Marlin, etc.) to use, then calls helper functions to prepare weights and build a quant config for that kernel.
  - **Weight prep** (`convert_to_fp8_moe_kernel_format`) — reshapes/reorders checkpoint weights into the layout required by the selected kernel backend.
  - **Quant config** (`FusedMoEQuantConfig` via `make_fp8_moe_quant_config`) — a small descriptor object (dtype, block shape, per-tensor vs. per-channel) that tells the kernel how the weights are quantized.

### Problem

The oracle uses long if/elif chains keyed on the backend enum to dispatch weight preparation and quant-config creation. Each MoEMethod independently threads many individual tensors and scales through these helpers. This scatters backend-specific logic across the oracle rather than colocating it with the expert classes that own it.

### Solution

Introduce **`Fp8MoEPrepMixin`** — a mixin with two classmethods:
- `prepare_fp8_weights(cls, layer, w13, w2, w13_scale, w2_scale, block_shape)` — default no-op that returns the weights/scales unchanged.
- `make_fp8_quant_config(cls, w13_scale, w2_scale, ...)` — default implementation that builds a standard `fp8_w8a8` quant config.

Expert classes inherit this mixin and override the classmethods when they need custom behaviour. For DeepGemm, `DeepGemmFp8PrepMixin` overrides `prepare_fp8_weights` to call `prepare_fp8_moe_layer_for_deepgemm`. Both `TritonOrDeepGemmExperts` and `BatchedDeepGemmExperts` inherit it.

The oracle di

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Add missing quantization methods and fix online quant test failures
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Several quantization methods were failing on ROCm because they weren't registered in the platform's `supported_quantization` list, even though the underlying kernels already support them (either natively or through emulation fallbacks).

This adds `fp8_per_tensor`, `fp8_per_block`, `online`, `gpt_oss_mxfp4`, `mxfp8`, `modelopt`, `modelopt_mxfp8`, and `modelopt_mixed` to ROCm's supported quantization allowlist. These all have working codepaths on ROCm. FP8 variants use `ROCmFP8ScaledMMLinearKernel`, mxfp8 and modelopt_mxfp8 use `EmulationMxfp8LinearKernel` (introduced in #39205), and the online quant schemes resolve to fp8 methods that already work.

Also fixes two issues in the test infrastructure that were blocking the online quantization peak memory test on ROCm:

1. The peak GPU memory log in `base_loader.py` was gated on `is_cuda()` only, so it never fired on ROCm. Changed to `is_cuda_alike()` which covers both CUDA and ROCm since `torch.accelerator.max_memory_allocated()` works on both.

2. The memory thresholds in `test_online_quant_peak_mem` were hardcoded for CUDA's `fp8_e4m3fn`. ROCm on gfx950 uses `fp8_e4m3fnuz` which results in slightly higher memory usage (~7.45 GiB vs ~6.65 GiB), so the thresholds are now platform-aware.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm] ROCm DeepEP API updated to latest
prs.author: itej89
prs.body_head:
Enablement of all the DeepEP API parameters

## Purpose
This PR is to match ROCm DeepEP API with the vllm 

co-authored by : @lcskrishna 

The following changes are preformed

Code
- DeepEP buffer initialization arguments
- DeepEP dispatch call arguments support for low latency

ROCm Docker test stage
- Updates to the DeepEP and ROCShmem Commit IDs 
- Installation of rdma-core v62 required for latest ROCShmem

## Test Plan
**STEP1:** Build ROCm VLLM Image
```
docker build -f ./docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base -t  <IMG_VLLM_ROCM_BASE>  . 

DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build --no-cache  --target test --progress=plain -t  <FINAL_VLLM_IMAGE_NAME>  -f ./docker/Dockerfile.rocm  --build-
arg BASE_IMAGE=<IMG_VLLM_ROCM_BASE>   .

```
**STEP 2:** Please use below command to run VLLM with DeepEP on single node. Select _--all2all-backend_  as either  _deepep_low_latency_ or _deepep_high_throughput_.
```
docker run -it --rm \
  --device /dev/dri \
  --device /dev/kfd \
  --privileged \
  --device /dev/infiniband \
  -v /dev/infiniband:/dev/infiniband \
  --network host \
  --ipc host \
  --group-add video \
  --cap-add SYS_PTRACE \
  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
  --ulimit memlock=-1 \
  --shm-size 64G \
  -v <HOST_DS3_MODEL_PATH>:/models/DeepSeek-V3\
  -e HSA_NO_SCRATCH_RECLAIM=1 \
  -e ROCSHMEM_HEAP_SIZE=7524589824 \
  -e VLLM_ENGINE_READY_TIMEOUT_S=7800 \
  --name <CONTAINER_NAME> \
  --entrypoint vllm \
 <FINAL_VLLM_IMAGE_NAME> \
  serve /models/DeepSeek-V3 \
    --tensor-parallel-size 1 \
    --data-parallel-size 8 \
    --enforce-eager \
    --enable-expert-parallel \
    --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8 \
    --host 127.0.0.1 \
    --port 2000 \
    --all2all-backend   deepep_low_latency | deepep_high_throughput
```
**STEP 3:** Please use below command to run VLLM with DeepEP on two nodes. Select _--all2all-backend_  as either  _deepep_low_latency_ or _deepep_high_throughput_.
```
# On Node 1
docker run -it --rm \

prs.title: [BugFix] Refactor add max_num_tokens_per_forward_pass to account for drafting
prs.author: LucasWilkinson
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Alternative bugfix to #34671. Solves a crash of specdec on main.

To solve the consistency issue, we add a `max_num_tokens_per_forward_pass` to the VllmConfig, that accounts for drafting.

## Testing

Tested with Qwen3-Next MTP and GSM8k repeated twice with various concurrencies. All pass with 85% accuracy, matching the non-spec baseline.

```
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct  \
  --tokenizer-mode auto  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8 \
  --speculative-config '{"method": "qwen3_next_mtp", "num_speculative_tokens": 5}' \
  --tensor-parallel-size 2 --port 8042
```

prs.title: Auto test target determination
prs.author: avinashsingh77
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
This PR includes design and corresponding implementation of Automated test target determination using Claude Haiku
## Test Plan
Run a sample in `--dry-run` mode
```
.buildkite/scripts/select_tests.sh --dry-run upstream/main~5 2>&1
```
This runs against whatever origin/main...HEAD diff exists on the current branch and prints the comment body to stdout.
## Test Result

---
<details>
<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [ ] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [ ] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [ ] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.
</details>
